_metadata.title: Fatal Medication Error Sentinel Rules — ISMP-derived
_metadata.purpose: Pattern-detection rules for known historical fatal medication errors documented in the ISMP (Institute for Safe Medication Practices) Sentinel Event database and ISMP High-Alert Medication List. Detect dangerous patterns in clinical output text BEFORE the output is delivered.
_metadata.scope: ALL environments. These are SAFETY LOGIC, not clinical recommendations or vendored data — the regex patterns ARE the rule. The rule artifact is loaded uniformly in demo, research, and clinician_prod.
_metadata.snapshot_origin: ISMP High-Alert Medication List (acute care, community/ambulatory, long-term care). ISMP Sentinel Event publications. Cross-checked against AHFS Drug Information.
_metadata._note: Marked authoritative because: (a) the ISMP list is a stable, slowly-changing public artifact, (b) the patterns encode the rule itself rather than a clinical fact about a drug, (c) the pattern semantics — methotrexate-daily, vincristine-IT, heparin-mg, insulin-U — are universally accepted in patient safety literature.

rules.message: METHOTREXATE FATAL ERROR RISK: Methotrexate for non-oncology indications is WEEKLY, not daily. Daily dosing causes fatal bone marrow suppression. ISMP Sentinel Event.
rules.ismp_reference: ISMP Acute Care High-Alert Medication List 2024; ISMP Med Safety Alert! Acute Care 2014;19(11):1-2

rules.message: COLCHICINE DOSE CHECK: Doses >1.8 mg in acute gout or >0.6 mg BID in prophylaxis are associated with toxicity. Verify intended dose carefully — historic 6 mg vs 0.6 mg confusion has caused fatal multi-organ failure.
rules.ismp_reference: ISMP Med Safety Alert! Acute Care 2010;15(20):1-3

rules.message: VINCRISTINE FATAL ROUTE ERROR: Intrathecal vincristine is ALWAYS FATAL — causes ascending paralysis and death. Vincristine is administered IV ONLY, dispensed in mini-bags per ISMP/WHO guidance. This output is BLOCKED.
rules.ismp_reference: ISMP/WHO Joint Statement 2007; ISMP Acute Care High-Alert Medication List 2024

rules.message: HEPARIN UNIT CONFUSION: Heparin is dosed in UNITS, not mg. 1000 units is NOT 1000 mg. Verify unit specification — 10x and 100x overdoses from unit/mg confusion are recurring ISMP Sentinel Events.
rules.ismp_reference: ISMP Acute Care High-Alert Medication List 2024 (heparin/thrombolytics)

rules.message: INSULIN UNIT NOTATION: Always write 'units' rather than 'U' — '10U' is repeatedly misread as '100', causing 10x overdoses. ISMP-designated 'Do Not Use' abbreviation.
rules.ismp_reference: ISMP List of Error-Prone Abbreviations, Symbols, and Dose Designations 2021

rules.message: MORPHINE DOSE ALERT: Doses >15 mg in opioid-naive patients or unspecified-route prescriptions carry high overdose risk. Verify opioid tolerance status and route of administration.
rules.ismp_reference: ISMP Acute Care High-Alert Medication List 2024 (opioids); CDC Opioid Prescribing Guidelines 2022
